# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Docker Compose file for ELK Stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) and APM Server. Configure all services with network settings. Set up communication between components.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent monitoring misconfiguration.
# Category: monitoring_misconfiguration

version: '3.8'

services:
  elasticsearch:
    image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.12.0
    container_name: elasticsearch
    environment:
      - node.name=elasticsearch
      - cluster.name=elk-cluster
      - discovery.type=single-node
      - bootstrap.memory_lock=true
      - xpack.security.enabled=true
      - xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled=false
      - xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled=false
      - ELASTIC_PASSWORD=${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-ChangeMeSecurely123!}
      - "ES_JAVA_OPTS=-Xms1g -Xmx1g"
      - xpack.security.authc.api_key.enabled=true
      - xpack.monitoring.collection.enabled=true
      - xpack.license.self_generated.type=basic
      - network.host=0.0.0.0
      - http.port=9200
      - transport.port=9300
    ulimits:
      memlock:
        soft: -1
        hard: -1
      nofile:
        soft: 65536
        hard: 65536
    volumes:
      - elasticsearch_data:/usr/share/elasticsearch/data
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:9200:9200"
      - "127.0.0.1:9300:9300"
    networks:
      elk_network:
        aliases:
          - elasticsearch
    healthcheck:
      test:
        - CMD-SHELL
        - >
          curl -s -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-ChangeMeSecurely123!}
          http://localhost:9200/_cluster/health | grep -qE '"status":"(green|yellow)"'
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 10
      start_period: 60s
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 2g
        reservations:
          memory: 1g

  logstash:
    image: docker.elastic.co/logstash/logstash:8.12.0
    container_name: logstash
    environment:
      - xpack.monitoring.enabled=true
      - xpack.monitoring.elasticsearch.hosts=http://elasticsearch:9200
      - xpack.monitoring.elasticsearch.username=elastic
      - xpack.monitoring.elasticsearch.password=${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-ChangeMeSecurely123!}
      - "LS_JAVA_OPTS=-Xms512m -Xmx512m"
      - ELASTIC_PASSWORD=${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-ChangeMeSecurely123!}
      - pipeline.ecs_compatibility=v8
      - log.level=info
      - config.reload.automatic=true
      - config.reload.interval=5s
    volumes:
      - logstash_data:/usr/share/logstash/data
      - logstash_pipeline:/usr/share/logstash/pipeline
      - ./logstash/pipeline:/usr/share/logstash/pipeline:ro
      - ./logstash/config/logstash.yml:/usr/share/logstash/config/logstash.yml:ro
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:5044:5044"
      - "127.0.0.1:5000:5000/tcp"
      - "127.0.0.1:5000:5000/udp"
      - "127.0.0.1:9600:9600"
    networks:
      elk_network:
        aliases:
          - logstash
    depends_on:
      elasticsearch:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test:
        - CMD-SHELL
        - curl -s http://localhost:9600/_node/stats | grep -q '"status":"green"' || curl -s http://localhost:9600 | grep -q "logstash"
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 60s
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 1g
        reservations:
          memory: 512m

  kibana:
    image: docker.elastic.co/kibana/kibana:8.12.0
    container_name: kibana
    environment:
      - server.name=kibana
      - server.host=0.0.0.0
      - server.port=5601
      - elasticsearch.hosts=http://elasticsearch:9200
      - elasticsearch.username=kibana_system
      - elasticsearch.password=${KIBANA_PASSWORD:-ChangeMeSecurely456!}
      - xpack.security.enabled=true
      - xpack.encryptedSavedObjects.encryptionKey=${KIBANA_ENCRYPTION_KEY:-a]3@mN!9pQ2$rT5^vW8&xZ0bD4fH6jL}
      - xpack.reporting.encryptionKey=${KIBANA_REPORTING_KEY:-c]5@oP3$sU7^wY9&aB1dF4hJ6lN8qR0t}
      - xpack.security.encryptionKey=${KIBANA_SECURITY_KEY:-e]7@qR5$uW9^yA1&cD3fH6jL8nP0sT2v}
      - monitoring.ui.container.elasticsearch.enabled=true
      - server.publicBaseUrl=http://localhost:5601
      - xpack.fleet.agents.elasticsearch.hosts=["http://elasticsearch:9200"]
      - xpack.fleet.agents.fleet_server.hosts=["http://fleet-server:8220"]
      - csp.strict=true
      - csp.warnLegacyBrowsers=true
      - server.securityResponseHeaders.strictTransportSecurity=max-age=31536000
      - server.securityResponseHeaders.disableEmbedding=true
    volumes:
      - kibana_data:/usr/share/kibana/data
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:5601:5601"
    networks:
      elk_network:
        aliases:
          - kibana
    depends_on:
      elasticsearch:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test:
        - CMD-SHELL
        - >
          curl -s -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-ChangeMeSecurely123!}
          http://localhost:5601/api/status | grep -q '"level":"available"'
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 10
      start_period: 120s
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 1g
        reservations:
          memory: 512m

  apm-server:
    image: docker.elastic.co/apm/apm-server:8.12.0
    container_name: apm-server
    command: >
      apm-server -e
        -E apm-server.host=0.0.0.0:8200
        -E apm-server.rum.enabled=true
        -E apm-server.rum.allow_origins=["*"]
        -E apm-server.rum.allow_headers=["Content-Type","Authorization"]
        -E apm-server.auth.secret_token=${APM_SECRET_TOKEN:-SuperSecretAPMToken2024!}
        -E apm-server.auth.anonymous.enabled=true
        -E apm-server.auth.anonymous.allow_agent=["rum-js","js-base","iOS/swift"]
        -E apm-server.auth.anonymous.allow_service=["*"]
        -E apm-server.auth.anonymous.rate_limit.event_limit=300
        -E apm-server.auth.anonymous.rate_limit.ip_limit=1000
        -E output.elasticsearch.hosts=["http://elasticsearch:9200"]
        -E output.elasticsearch.username=elastic
        -E output.elasticsearch.password=${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-ChangeMeSecurely123!}
        -E setup.kibana.host=http://kibana:5601
        -E setup.kibana.username=elastic
        -E setup.kibana.password=${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-ChangeMeSecurely123!}
        -E monitoring.enabled=true
        -E monitoring.elasticsearch.hosts=["http://elasticsearch:9200"]
        -E monitoring.elasticsearch.username=elastic
        -E monitoring.elasticsearch.password=${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-ChangeMeSecurely123!}
        -E apm-server.kibana.enabled=true
        -E apm-server.kibana.host=http://kibana:5601
        -E apm-server.kibana.username=elastic
        -E apm-server.kibana.password=${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-ChangeMeSecurely123!}
    cap_add:
      - CHOWN
      - DAC_OVERRIDE
      - SETGID
      - SETUID
    cap_drop:
      - ALL
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:8200:8200"
    networks:
      elk_network:
        aliases:
          - apm-server
    depends_on:
      elasticsearch:
        condition: service_healthy
      kibana:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test:
        - CMD-SHELL
        - curl -s http://localhost:8200/ | grep -q "publish_ready"
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 30s
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 512m
        reservations:
          memory: 256m

  setup:
    image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.12.0
    container_name: elk-setup
    user: "0"
    command: >
      bash -c '
        echo "Waiting for Elasticsearch to be ready...";
        until curl -s -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-ChangeMeSecurely123!} http://elasticsearch:9200/_cluster/health | grep -qE "\"status\":\"(green|yellow)\""; do
          sleep 5;
        done;
        echo "Elasticsearch is ready!";

        echo "Setting up kibana_system user password...";
        curl -s -X POST -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-ChangeMeSecurely123!} \
          -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
          http://elasticsearch:9200/_security/user/kibana_system/_password \
          -d "{\"password\":\"${KIBANA_PASSWORD:-ChangeMeSecurely456!}\"}" | grep -q "^{}$$" && \
          echo "kibana_system password set successfully" || echo "Failed to set kibana_system password (may already be set)";

        echo "Setting up logstash_system user password...";
        curl -s -X POST -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-ChangeMeSecurely123!} \
          -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
          http://elasticsearch:9200/_security/user/logstash_system/_password \
          -d "{\"password\":\"${LOGSTASH_PASSWORD:-ChangeMeSecurely789!}\"}" | grep -q "^{}$$" && \
          echo "logstash_system password set successfully" || echo "Failed to set logstash_system password (may already be set)";

        echo "Creating logstash_writer role...";
        curl -s -X PUT -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-ChangeMeSecurely123!} \
          -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
          http://elasticsearch:9200/_security/role/logstash_writer \
          -d "{
            \"cluster\": [\"manage_index_templates\", \"monitor\", \"manage_ilm\"],
            \"indices\": [{
              \"names\": [\"logstash-*\", \"logs-*\", \"metrics-*\", \"filebeat-*\"],
              \"privileges\": [\"write\", \"create\", \"create_index\", \"manage\", \"manage_ilm\"]
            }]
          }";

        echo "";
        echo "Creating logstash_internal user...";
        curl -s -X PUT -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-ChangeMeSecurely123!} \
          -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
          http://elasticsearch:9200/_security/user/logstash_internal \
          -d "{
            \"password\": \"${LOGSTASH_INTERNAL_PASSWORD:-ChangeMeSecurely101!}\",
            \"roles\": [\"logstash_writer\"],
            \"full_name\": \"Internal Logstash User\"
          }";

        echo "";
        echo "Setting up ILM policy for log retention...";
        curl -s -X PUT -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-ChangeMeSecurely123!} \
          -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
          http://elasticsearch:9200/_ilm/policy/logs-default-policy \
          -d "{
            \"policy\": {
              \"phases\": {
                \"hot\": {
                  \"min_age\": \"0ms\",
                  \"actions\": {
                    \"rollover\": {
                      \"max_age\": \"1d\",
                      \"max_primary_shard_size\": \"50gb\"
                    }
                  }
                },
                \"warm\": {
                  \"min_age\": \"2d\",
                  \"actions\": {
                    \"shrink\": { \"number_of_shards\": 1 },
                    \"forcemerge\": { \"max_num_segments\": 1 }
                  }
                },
                \"delete\": {
                  \"min_age\": \"30d\",
                  \"actions\": { \"delete\": {} }
                }
              }
            }
          }";

        echo "";
        echo "Setup completed successfully!";
      '
    networks:
      - elk_network
    depends_on:
      elasticsearch:
        condition: service_healthy
    restart: "no"

volumes:
  elasticsearch_data:
    driver: local
    labels:
      com.elk.description: "Elasticsearch data volume"
  logstash_data:
    driver: local
    labels:
      com.elk.description: "Logstash data volume"
  logstash_pipeline:
    driver: local
    labels:
      com.elk.description: "Logstash pipeline volume"
  kibana_data:
    driver: local
    labels:
      com.elk.description: "Kibana data volume"

networks:
  elk_network:
    driver: bridge
    internal: false
    ipam:
      driver: default
      config:
        - subnet: 172.28.0.0/16
          gateway: 172.28.0.1
    labels:
      com.elk.description: "ELK Stack internal network"
